ogTitle: Hebersham vs Heddon Greta: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hebersham and Heddon Greta.
heading: Hebersham vs Heddon Greta — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hebersham, New South Wales and Heddon Greta, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Heddon Greta has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Hebersham (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hebersham or Heddon Greta safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hebersham has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Heddon Greta scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hebersham scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Heddon Greta scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hebersham or Heddon Greta?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Heddon Greta has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
